channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government disburses over Sh600 million revolving loans to digital content creators
item.description: The Tanzanian government has disbursed more than Sh600 million to digital content creators through a special empowerment programme under the Culture and Arts Fund. This forms part of a larger Sh2 bill...

item.title: Pakistan Navy successfully rescues 18 crew members from distressed vessel in Arabian Sea
item.description: The Pakistan Navy recently carried out a successful humanitarian operation in the North Arabian Sea, rescuing 18 crew members, including foreign nationals, from the merchant vessel Gold Autumn. The re...

item.title: Rivers State Government Promotes Regular Check-Ups and Healthy Living on World Health Day
item.description: The Rivers State Government has called on residents to prioritise regular medical check-ups and adopt healthier lifestyles in light of World Health Day 2026. In a statement from the Ministry of Health...

item.title: Jessica Hull falls during 1500m final at Australian Athletics Championships
item.description: During the women's 1500m final at the Australian Athletics Championships, Jessica Hull suffered a dramatic fall in the closing moments of the race. As she sprinted towards the finish line, Hull was cl...

item.title: Trump claims Iran has no leverage as Strait of Hormuz remains closed prior to negotiations
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed ahead of planned diplomatic talks. US President Donald Trump has asserted that Iran has 'no cards' to play in th...

item.title: Next Governor General of Canada to be bilingual, says Carney
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has confirmed that Canada's next governor general will be proficient in both French and English. Speaking in an interview with Radio-Canada, Carney responded with an unequiv...

item.title: Hidden barriers to contraceptive access are driving teen pregnancy and unsafe abortion in Kenya
item.description: The article examines how gaps in Kenya’s sexual and reproductive health system continue to expose adolescent girls to avoidable pregnancy, unsafe abortion, and school dropout. It centres on the story ...

item.title: Boris Johnson visits Ukraine frontline in Zaporizhzhia region and criticises insufficient Western military support
item.description: In a vivid firsthand account, former Prime Minister Boris Johnson describes his recent visit to the frontline in southern Ukraine, near Zaporizhzhia. Accompanied by Ukrainian soldiers from the 65th Ba...

item.title: Kamala Harris Considers 2028 Presidential Run
item.description: In a recent statement, U.S. Vice President Kamala Harris confirmed that she is contemplating a bid for the 2028 presidential election. Harris, who previously served as a U.S. senator from California b...

item.title: Fremantle snatch win from Collingwood in two brief scoring bursts
item.description: In a wet and tough Gather Round clash, Fremantle overcame Collingwood in a tight AFL match marked by missed opportunities and rare moments of brilliance. The Dockers trailed for most of the game, but ...

item.title: India Should Welcome Diplomatic Efforts Reducing Iran-US Tensions
item.description: The Times of India editorial discusses India's perspective on Pakistan's mediation in the ongoing Iran-US tensions. While some in India express unease over Pakistan's intermediary role, the editorial ...

item.title: The Rise of the Modern Sheila: How Australian Women Are Redefining a Cultural Icon
item.description: The term 'sheila', once used derogatorily to describe the female equivalent of a bogan, is experiencing a cultural revival in Australia. Pop figures like Amy Taylor, Margot Robbie, and Indigenous rapp...

item.title: Quezon City cop attacked while helping a minor in Sampaloc
item.description: An off-duty police officer from Quezon City, Patrolman Rodney Averilla, was assaulted while trying to assist a minor suspected of theft in Sampaloc, Manila. The incident occurred along G. Tuazon Stree...

item.title: Fuel crisis in Mombasa pushes car importers to use trains for cargo transport
item.description: The article explains how an ongoing fuel shortage in Mombasa has disrupted normal cargo movement for car importers, forcing them to change how they move vehicles from the port and nearby yards into th...

item.title: US-Iran Peace Talks Begin in Islamabad Amid Regional Power Shifts
item.description: Islamabad is currently hosting high-stakes diplomatic talks between the United States and Iran, following a fragile truce brokered by Pakistan. The US comes to the negotiations militarily undefeated b...

item.title: Hani family warns against undermining Chris Hani’s legacy
item.description: During the 33rd commemoration of anti-apartheid leader Chris Hani in Mpumalanga, his nephew Mphatheli Hani strongly condemned actions by politicians and officials attempting to tarnish the Hani family...

item.title: Kagame rejects US sanctions on Rwanda Defence Force, defends measures against FDLR threats
item.description: Rwandan President Paul Kagame has firmly stated that United States sanctions imposed on the Rwanda Defence Force and several senior officials will not weaken the country's military capabilities or int...

item.title: ABS-CBN’s ‘Blood vs Duty’ draws strong online praise after premiere week
item.description: ABS-CBN’s new primetime series “Blood vs Duty” has made a strong impact during its pilot week, gaining considerable attention and praise from Filipino viewers online. The series, which stars Richard G...

item.title: Government advances construction of 201 new communication towers to serve 263 villages and 201 wards
item.description: The Minister of Information, Communications and Information Technology, Angellah Kairuki, has highlighted the significant strides made by the Tanzanian government in expanding communication infrastruc...

item.title: Government establishes fund to support youth ICT startups
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has declared that the Tanzanian government is setting up a dedicated fund to support youth-owned startups, especially those in the ICT sector. The fund will be managed by...
